heading: Certification of embryo laboratories
label: 263a–2 Certification of embryo laboratories
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Fertility Clinic Success Rate and Certification Act of 1992, and not as part of the Public Health Service Act which comprises this chapter.
Change of NameCenters for Disease Control changed to Centers for Disease Control and Prevention by , , .Pub. L. 102–531, title III, § 312106 Stat. 3504Oct. 27, 1992
Effective DateSection effective upon expiration of 2 years after , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 24, 1992section 9 of Pub. L. 102–493section 263a–1 of this title
amendments: Change of NameCenters for Disease Control changed to Centers for Disease Control and Prevention by , , .Pub. L. 102–531, title III, § 312106 Stat. 3504Oct. 27, 1992

children.heading: Development
children.label: 1 Development
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary, through the Centers for Disease Control, shall develop a model program for the certification of embryo laboratories (referred to in this section as a “certification program”) to be carried out by the States.October 24, 1992

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In developing the certification program under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consult with appropriate consumer and professional organizations with expertise in using, providing, and evaluating professional services and embryo laboratories associated with the assisted reproductive technology programs.
the Governor of each State,
the presiding officers of each State legislature,
the public health official of each State, and
the official responsible in each State for the operation of the State’s contract with the Secretary under ,section 1395aa of this title

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 1 Administration
children.text: The certification program shall be administered by the State and shall provide for the inspection and certification of embryo laboratories in the State by the State or by approved accreditation organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: assurances satisfactory to the State that the embryo laboratory will be operated in accordance with the standards under subsection (d),

children.label: B
children.text: a report to the State identifying the assisted reproductive technology programs with which the laboratory is associated, and

children.label: C
children.text: such other information as the State finds necessary.

A standard to assure consistent performance of procedures by each embryo laboratory certified under the certification program or by an approved accreditation organization in a State which has not adopted the certification program.
A standard for a quality assurance and a quality control program to assure valid, reliable, and reproduceable procedures in the laboratory.1So in original. Probably should be “reproducible”.1

A standard for the maintenance of records (on a program by program basis) on laboratory tests and procedures performed, including the scientific basis of, and the methodology used for, the tests, procedures, and preparation of any standards or controls, criteria for acceptable and unacceptable outcomes, criteria for sample rejection, and procedures for safe sample disposal.

A standard for the maintenance of written records on personnel and facilities necessary for proper and effective operation of the laboratory, schedules of preventive maintenance, function verification for equipment, and the release of such records to the State upon demand.
A standard for the use of such personnel who meet such qualifications as the Secretary may develop.
assurances by the State satisfactory to the Secretary that the certification program within the State meets the requirements of this section,
an agreement to make such reports as the Secretary may require, and
information about any proposed use of accreditation organizations under subsection (g).2So in original. Probably should be subsection “(f)”.2

children.heading: Use of accreditation organizations
children.label: f Use of accreditation organizations
children.text: A State which has adopted the certification program may use accreditation organizations approved under to inspect and certify embryo laboratories.section 263a–3 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A State which qualifies to adopt the certification program within the State shall conduct inspections in accordance with paragraph (2) to determine if laboratories in the State meet the requirements of such program. Such inspections shall be carried out by the State or by accreditation organizations used by the State under subsection (g).2

children.label: A
children.text: be periodic and unannounced, or

children.label: B
children.text: be announced in such circumstances as the Secretary determines will not diminish the likelihood of discovering deficiencies in the operations of a laboratory.

children.heading: Results
children.label: 3 Results
children.text: The specific findings, including deficiencies, identified in an inspection carried out under paragraph (1) and any subsequent corrections to those deficiencies shall be announced and made available to the public upon request beginning no later than 60 days after the date of the inspection.

children.label: A
children.text: which have been certified by a State under the certification program, or

children.label: B
children.text: which have been certified by an accreditation organization approved by the Secretary under ,section 263a–3 of this title

children.heading: Access to facilities and records
children.label: 2 Access to facilities and records
children.text: In conducting an inspection of an embryo laboratory under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall have access to all facilities, equipment, materials, records, and information which the Secretary determines is necessary to determine if such laboratory is being operated in accordance with the standards in subsection (d). As part of such an inspection, the Secretary may copy any material, record, or information inspected or require it to be submitted to the Secretary. Such an inspection may be made only upon the presentation of identification to the owner, operator, or agent in charge of the laboratory being inspected.

children.label: A
children.text: notify the State in which the laboratory is located and, if appropriate, the accreditation organization which certified the laboratory,

children.label: B
children.text: make available to the public the results of the inspection,

children.label: i
children.text: such State in carrying out the certification program is reliably identifying the deficiencies of such laboratory, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the accreditation organization which certified such laboratories is reliably identifying such deficencies, and3So in original. Probably should be “deficiencies,”.3

children.label: i
children.text: that such State in carrying out the certification program has not met the requirements applicable to such program, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the accreditation organization which certified such laboratory has not met the requirements of ,section 263a–3 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 1 Secretary
children.text: In developing the certification program, the Secretary may not establish any regulation, standard, or requirement which has the effect of exercising supervision or control over the practice of medicine in assisted reproductive technology programs.

children.heading: State
children.label: 2 State
children.text: In adopting the certification program, a State may not establish any regulation, standard, or requirement which has the effect of exercising supervision or control over the practice of medicine in assisted reproductive technology programs.

children.heading: Term
children.label: j Term
children.text: The term of a certification issued by a State or an accreditation organization in a State shall be prescribed by the Secretary in the certification program and shall be valid for a period of time to be defined by the Secretary through the public comment process described in subsection (h)(2). The Secretary shall provide an application for recertification to be submitted at the time of changes in the ownership of a certified laboratory or changes in the administration of such a laboratory.4So in original. Probably should be subsection “(g)(2)”.4
